beats.activity: Wake up, check on rooftop herb garden in the drizzle, make coffee.
beats.reasoning: Mei's routine: morning ritual with her rooftop garden and coffee. Even in grey weather, she checks on her basil and mint. Yesterday's low energy (35/100) suggests she'll keep this beat grounded and short—just tending plants and caffeine before heading out.

beats.activity: Commute to shipyard in Interbay, arriving before rain intensifies.
beats.reasoning: Steady rain 7am–2pm; Mei leaves early to avoid the worst of it and beats the 9am SR-520 eastbound closure (which won't affect her westbound Ballard→Interbay route). 40 min allows for wet conditions.

beats.activity: Welding shift at the shipyard; steady work on hull repairs.
beats.reasoning: Full morning/early-afternoon shift at her primary job. Rain outside but she's indoors at the torch. Wraps before the afternoon peak of the storm.

beats.activity: Lunch break at a nearby café; grab soup and bread to stay warm.
beats.reasoning: Rainy day calls for hot food. Staying local near the shipyard rather than venturing across town—frugal choice, and the rain is still steady until 2pm.

beats.activity: Return to shipyard for afternoon shift; finish welding jobs.
beats.reasoning: Second half of her shift. Rain clears by 2pm, so evening commute home will be dry. Solid day of work with no weather disruptions.
Rain has cleared by now. 40 min westbound from Interbay to Ballard, no bridge closures affecting this route.

beats.activity: Quick shower and change; prep dinner ingredients from the garden.
beats.reasoning: Post-work wind-down. Mei is a night owl and low-energy after yesterday; she'll keep the evening low-key. Gathers herbs from the rooftop for dinner.

beats.activity: Cook and eat simple stir-fry with fresh herbs; reflect on the day.
beats.reasoning: Continuing her pattern of simple, herb-rich meals at home. Frugal, grounded, and fits her night-owl rhythm.

beats.activity: Text with Jasmine about weekend plans; journal and tea on the couch.
beats.reasoning: Mei values close friends; a light check-in with Jasmine fits her social style. Then quiet evening leisure—journaling and tea align with her reflective, introverted recharge after a rainy work day.

diary: Woke up early to check on the herbs—basil and cilantro are doing surprisingly well despite the drizzle. The rain was steady all morning, which meant I caught my commute before things got really ugly. Heard the 520 bridge was a nightmare, but I dodged that mess. The shipyard was its usual rhythm: metal, sparks, the smell of salt water mixing with welding fumes. Grabbed soup at Delancey around lunch and just sat there warming my hands on the bowl, watching the rain sheet down the café windows. Back to work, finished a hull job that's been nagging me all week. By the time I got home, I was ready to just exist quietly. Showered off the day, threw together a stir-fry with my garden herbs—there's something grounding about cooking with things you've grown yourself. Texted Jasmine about maybe hitting the market this weekend. Now I'm on the couch with tea and my journal, listening to the rain ease up outside. Tired but settled.
thought_process: I decided to lean into the rhythm of the day rather than fight it. The weather was grey and the bridge closure meant chaos for a lot of people, but I got lucky with my route and timing—I took that as a sign to just move through the day steadily. Work felt good today, purposeful. I think the rain put me in a reflective mood, made me want to slow down. Using the fresh herbs from my garden for dinner felt like a small way of bringing something intentional into an otherwise ordinary Tuesday. Jasmine's been busy, so I'm hoping we can actually make the market happen. The evening routine of tea and journaling helped me process the day without overthinking it.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
